title: salix phylicifolia var. elliptica
intro: Salix phylicifolia var. elliptica, also known as the small-leaved willow, is a variety of shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia var. elliptica include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and fever, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from headaches and colds to skin conditions and digestive problems.
